title: §6.4 adaptive-λ operator advisor (lambda_advisor) — retire the stale §6.3 rung_advisor
source: Launch-day finding (this session): rung_advisor implements the SUPERSEDED §6.3 capped sleeve
why: scripts/rung_advisor.py (backed by tournament_sim/rung_policy.py) implements the SUPERSEDED §6.3 capped-momentum sleeve: it recommends --lottery-risk-frac (the wrong instrument) + a coupled --portfolio-kill, and can recommend FLAT. The RATIFIED posture is the §6.4 adaptive-λ static-long-crypto bet sized by --stage2-lambda (λ∈[1.5,2.4]), §14-safe by sizing, rule = NEVER flat. Running rung_advisor at an R1/R2 close would hand the operator the wrong instrument and could say 'go FLAT' (forbidden by §6.4). The §6.4 mechanical table lived only as runbook markdown — no executable advisor mirrored it.
value_rationale: First live λ decision is the R1 close (~Jun 22 22:00). A correct, executable advisor that shares ONE pure rule with the sim (the principle that made rung_policy shared) removes live judgment and prevents a wrong-instrument / flat recommendation at the cut.
scope: NEW tournament_sim/lambda_policy.py: pure §6.4 policy (no I/O/clocks/rng), transcribed EXACTLY from runbook §6.4 lines 362-373; (d,q,µ)->λ table, R1=1.5 blind, pre-final non-adaptive (2.0 / 1.5-lead / never flat), ceiling 2.4, margin_div<30 re-scale DOWN only.
NEW scripts/lambda_advisor.py: operator CLI; prints --stage2-lambda + the §6.1 relaunch command; NEVER prints --lottery-risk-frac or FLAT; ASCII-safe for the cp1252 console.
scripts/rung_advisor.py: loud deprecation banner -> redirect to §6.4/lambda_advisor (rung_policy left intact; still imported by tournament_sim/run.py + sims).

scripts/simulate_barbell.py: docstring-only xref on policy_lambda naming lambda_policy authoritative + documenting the ONE-corner deviation (sim floors µ<0 lenient-harvest at 0.1; runbook+policy floor 0.4).
recommendation: Built by execution-engineer (TDD, 33 tests green; full suite 2904 green). Reviewed vs runbook §6.4 — faithful. ESCALATE to CEO/risk: the sim-vs-runbook µ<0 floor (0.1 vs 0.4) — live advisor uses runbook-exact 0.4 (more consistent with NEVER-flat), sim numerics untouched on launch night; immaterial to P(top25) (the corner is 'comfortable + field dying'). Operator passes --margin-div (B0195) at each round close for the §14 re-scale.
out_of_scope: Changing the validated sim policy_lambda numerics; the delta-rebalance roll MECHANISM (B0203); which λ to actually pick (CEO/operator reads the table).
